title: Vault scan semantics are under-specified: `.md` case sensitivity, sub-directories, symlinks, and dot-prefixed files
description:
REQ-FEED-028 step 1 says: '`std::fs::read_dir(vault_path)` で `.md` ファイルを収集する'. Step 2 says: '拡張子を除いたファイル名を `HashSet<String>` に格納する'. Unanswered: (a) Case sensitivity — does `foo.MD` (uppercase) count as a `.md` file? The current implementation at feed.rs:398 uses `ext == "md"` which is case-sensitive on the Rust side, but macOS HFS+/APFS default is case-insensitive at the filesystem level, so a file authored as `bar.md` could be returned by `read_dir` with the case the user typed. The spec is silent. (b) Subdirectories — `read_dir` is non-recursive; REQ-FEED-022 (line 596) is explicit ('non-recursive, same as fs_list_markdown') but REQ-FEED-028 does not re-state this. (c) Symlinks — `read_dir` follows symlinks for `metadata()` but not for entry path; if a symlink points outside the vault, is its stem in `existing_ids`? (d) Dot-prefixed files like `.foo.md` — included? (e) Files with multiple extensions like `foo.bak.md` — `file_stem()` returns `foo.bak`, while a naive split-on-last-dot returns `foo.bak` as well; consistent, but the spec doesn't address whether the stem includes intermediate dots. (f) Unicode normalization — if `note_id` 採番 produces an ASCII-only `2026-05-13-...` and existing files have NFC vs. NFD filenames, the collision check is moot (the new ID is ASCII), but if a future sprint introduces non-ASCII IDs, the spec's silence will bite. 

These are not all critical, but the spec asserts 'O(n) で完了' (line 935) without defining what 'n' is — n = number of `.md` files however the implementer chooses to count them.
evidence:
behavioral-spec.md:919 — '`std::fs::read_dir(vault_path)` で `.md` ファイルを収集する' (no case rule, no recursion rule)
promptnotes/src-tauri/src/feed.rs:398 — `if file_path.extension().map_or(false, |ext| ext == "md")` (case-sensitive, but spec doesn't require this)
behavioral-spec.md:596 — REQ-FEED-022 says 'non-recursive' explicitly; Sprint 5 does not inherit or restate this
remediation: Add a 'Vault Scan Semantics' subsection to REQ-FEED-028 specifying: 'Extension check is case-insensitive (`.md`, `.MD`, `.Md` all match) — OR — case-sensitive (only `.md`). Scan is non-recursive (does not descend into subdirectories). Symlinks are NOT followed. Dot-prefixed files are EXCLUDED. File stem is computed via `Path::file_stem` (everything before the last dot in the basename).' Pick a position for each and write it down. Without these decisions, the spec admits implementations that differ in observable behavior (e.g., on a vault with `2026-05-13-040000-000.MD` already present, a case-insensitive impl will pick `-1` suffix while a case-sensitive impl will not, producing different ghost IDs).
